physicalDetails.detail: Tall laborer with a limp, placed inside ordinary yard work but carrying himself with the wrong kind of alertness for a common dock hand.
Notably does not sweat in the trapped district heat and keeps more attention on exits than incoming loads.

goalsAtChapterEnd.active: Not directly shown on page; by behavior he appears to be maintaining cell security through watchfulness and lane control.
goalsAtChapterEnd.underlying: Likely to protect the hidden structure in the yards rather than perform ordinary labor first.

emotionalStateAtChapterEnd.state: Not directly available on page; the visible register is disciplined vigilance rather than readable feeling.

decisionsThisChapter.detail: Checks the alley mouth repeatedly instead of the incoming team, marking himself as a watcher rather than a normal yard laborer.

abilitiesDemonstrated.ability: Maintains covert watch behavior under civilian cover well enough that only sustained attention reveals the mismatch.

howOthersAddressHim.speaker: Aldric Havel
howOthersAddressHim.pattern: Names him as Teren once the pattern on him is strong enough to say aloud.

bodyLanguagePatterns.detail: Checks exits, not loads; stands in labor space with the readiness of a man expecting trouble instead of work.

knowledge.detail: Appears to know inspector shifts and alley visibility better than an ordinary dock transfer should.

arc.currentPosition: Chapter 7 introduces Teren as a likely operative inside the Andorhal cell rather than a developed interior character.
